steps.title: Test the predator claim
steps.content: Costa and colleagues claim the caterpillar sounds are directed primarily at predators. Strong support should show that the sounds happen in response to attack-like conditions rather than as ordinary communication.

steps.title: Use timing evidence
steps.content: B concerns whether species make distinctive sounds, D concerns how many sounds each species tends to make, and C supports only some sound types being detectable by predators. The strongest finding ties sound production tightly to simulated attacks. The answer is A.
